faqs.a: Self-harm means deliberately hurting your own body as a way to deal with emotional pain, stress, or feelings that have become too much to hold. It's often a way of trying to cope, to feel something when numb, or to release pressure that feels unbearable.

faqs.q: Is self-harm the same as wanting to end your life?
faqs.a: Not always. Self-harm is usually about managing pain in the moment and isn't always the same as wanting to end one's life, though both deserve care and compassion. If you're thinking about suicide or are in immediate danger, please reach out right away.

faqs.q: Where can I get support if I'm struggling with self-harm?
faqs.a: Support is available and things can get better. A mental health professional can help you understand what's driving the pain and build coping skills that work. In the US, you can call or text 988 any time to reach the Suicide and Crisis Lifeline, and you don't have to face this alone.
